description:
lib.rs:68 uses split_once('/') and then explicitly tests both halves for is_empty(). This handles three error cases — no slash, leading slash (empty service), trailing slash (empty account) — with three distinct error messages. A 'simplification' to use a single regex or to skip the empty checks would silently accept malformed URIs.

The nested-account test (lib.rs:202) confirms account may contain additional slashes; this is correct because Secret Service / macOS Keychain account names commonly do.
title: Defend: split_once('/') for service/account, with explicit empty checks
